title: Herbal Tinctures for Chills: 8 Proven Remedies

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate body aches, making them a natural remedy to soothe and calm the body when it's feeling cold and uncomfortable.
Quercetin is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fever and relieve body aches, allowing the body to recover from illness more quickly.
Iridoids are good for chills because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and relax the body, making them effective in relieving cold and flu symptoms.
Cichoric acid is good for chills because it helps boost the immune system by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections that cause the body to feel cold.
Caffeic acid is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and calm the body's immune response, making it easier to shake off chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a herbal remedy that can help with chills. This is because it contains compounds that boost the immune system. When we get chills, it's usually a sign that our body is fighting off an infection. Echinacea purpurea tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease symptoms. It also has antioxidants that protect cells from damage caused by free radicals. Some people take Echinacea purpurea tincture to prevent illness and reduce the severity of symptoms when they do get sick. The tincture can be taken as a supplement, often mixed with water or added to food. It's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea tincture may interact with certain medications, and its effectiveness can vary from person to person.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture, which helps with chills, start by gathering fresh or dried elder flowers, leaves, or berries. Clean and dry the plant material thoroughly, then place it in a clean glass jar. Next, pour a neutral spirit, such as vodka or brandy, over the plant material, making sure that the liquid completely covers the herbs. The general ratio is to use one part plant material to five parts spirit. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day for two to four weeks to help the plant material infuse its properties into the spirit. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, dilute the tincture with water to a concentration of 1:5 or 1:10, depending on your preference, and use it as needed to help alleviate chills and other symptoms associated with colds and flu.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for chills because it helps to reduce inflammation and increase body temperature, which makes it easier to shake off the feeling of being cold.

Shogaols are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the body's response to cold temperatures and alleviate the discomfort associated with chills.
Zingerone is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce body temperature and relieve cold-related discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. When made into a tincture, it can be a powerful remedy for chills. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce fever and soothe the body. When taken internally, ginger tincture can help to warm the body from the inside out, reducing the feeling of coldness and discomfort. Additionally, ginger has antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off underlying infections that may be causing the chills. This can be especially helpful during the winter months when colds and flu are more common. By taking ginger tincture, you can help to ease your chills and feel more comfortable, allowing your body to focus on recovering from illness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tinctures that help with chills, you will need Sambucus nigra flowers, a clean glass jar, vodka or glycerin as a solvent, and a clean glass dropper. First, dry the Sambucus nigra flowers completely, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator. Then, fill the glass jar about 1/4 of the way with the dried flowers. Next, pour the vodka or glycerin over the flowers, making sure that the flowers are completely covered. The ratio of flowers to solvent is typically 1 part flowers to 2 parts solvent, but you can adjust this to your liking. Stir the mixture well and seal the jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the active ingredients from the flowers. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and use the liquid tincture as needed, typically by taking 20-30 drops in water or tea to help with chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is often used to help with chills. This is because the plant has natural properties that can help improve blood flow and warm up the body. The tincture contains flavonoids and terpenoids that can help increase blood circulation and open up blood vessels. This can be especially helpful for people who get chills due to poor circulation. The increased blood flow can also help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with chills. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals that can make you feel cold. Taking Ginkgo biloba tincture may help to warm up the body from the inside out, making it a potential remedy for people who experience chills.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tinctures that help with chills, start by gathering dried elderberry flowers and berries, as Sambucus nigra is also known. Next, combine 1 part of the dried herbs with 5 parts of a high-proof spirit, such as vodka or brandy, in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is filled to the top to prevent air from getting in, which can damage the herbs. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. You can now use the Sambucus nigra tincture as is, or dilute it with water to the desired strength. When using it to combat chills, take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a liquid extract made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree. It is often used to help relieve chills, which are a common symptom of colds and flu. The tincture works by releasing the oil from the eucalyptus leaves, which contains a compound called eucalyptol. Eucalyptol is a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory that helps to open up the airways and reduce congestion. When you take eucalyptus globulus tincture, the eucalyptol is absorbed into your body and helps to break down mucus and reduce the severity of chills. The tincture can also help to reduce fever and alleviate body aches associated with chills. It is often taken in small doses, mixed with water or other liquids, and can be used in combination with other remedies to help manage symptoms of colds and flu.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture that helps with chills, you will need dried elder flowers, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like glycerin or vodka, and a clean glass dropper. First, measure out one part of dried elder flowers and two parts of your chosen carrier oil in the glass jar. Next, fill the jar the rest of the way with the carrier oil. Place the lid on the jar and let it sit in a cool dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called "maceration," and it allows the medicinal properties of the elder flowers to infuse into the oil. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Now your Sambucus nigra herbal tincture is ready to use. Take a few drops of the tincture under the tongue or mix it with water to help soothe chills and other symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tincture is made from the bark of the Cinchona tree. It contains a special compound called quinine, which helps to fight off infections that can cause chills. When we get chills, it's usually because our body is trying to fight off a fever or an infection. Quinine in Cinchona officinalis tincture helps to reduce the fever and make our body feel better. It also helps to relieve pain and discomfort that often comes with chills. Some people also find that it helps to calm their body and mind, making them feel more relaxed. By reducing the fever and infection, Cinchona officinalis tincture can help to alleviate chills and help the body recover faster. It's also often used to treat other symptoms like sweating, headaches, and muscle aches that often come with chills.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture that helps with chills, you will need fresh or dried elderberry flowers, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil such as vodka or glycerin, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. First, gather or purchase the Sambucus nigra flowers and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris. If using fresh flowers, dry them in a warm, dark place for a few days until they are completely dry and crumbly. Fill the glass jar about 2/3 full with the dried flowers and pour the carrier oil over the flowers, making sure that the flowers are completely covered. Secure the lid and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Take a few drops of the tincture under the tongue or add it to water to help alleviate chills and other symptoms of colds and flu.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is a herbal remedy that can help with chills. It contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce body temperature and ease shivering. When you take the tincture, the glycyrrhizin works to calm your body's response to infection or illness, which can cause chills. The tincture can also help to support your immune system, making it easier for your body to fight off the underlying cause of the chills. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can also contribute to a decrease in body temperature and a reduction in chills. The tincture can be taken as needed, usually in small doses, and it's often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture that helps with chills, you'll need some fresh or dried elder flowers, a jar, a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, a carrier oil like glycerin or vodka, and a clean glass measuring cup. First, gather a handful of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers or 1/4 cup of dried flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently rinse them with clean water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. Next, place the flowers in the clean glass jar and pour enough carrier oil to cover the flowers completely, usually around 2-3 cups. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. After the sitting period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your Sambucus nigra tincture, which can be taken in small amounts to help alleviate chills. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as needed.

Astragalus membranaceus tincture can also help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy balance of bodily fluids, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness. Some people find that taking Astragalus membranaceus tincture can help to alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu, which often cause chills. Additionally, it may help to regulate body temperature, making it easier to feel warm and comfortable.

Start by drying the elderberry flowers completely, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator. Once they're dry, place the flowers in the glass jar, making sure they're packed tightly. Then, pour the vodka or glycerin over the flowers, making sure they're completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great remedy for chills due to their ability to stimulate the immune system and provide natural relief from discomfort. Echinacea purpurea, also known as coneflower, is a popular herb in herbal medicine that helps to boost the immune system and fight off infections. It's particularly effective in reducing the severity and duration of chills, as well as other cold and flu symptoms. Meanwhile, Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a sore throat and calm the body, making it easier to shake off chills. By combining these herbs into a tincture, you can create a powerful remedy that not only alleviates chills but also helps to prevent them from happening in the first place. Fixing the issue of chills is important because it can improve your quality of life by reducing the number of days spent feeling unwell. When you're not bogged down by chills, you can focus on enjoying life's activities, spending time with loved ones, and pursuing your passions. By incorporating herbal tinctures into your health routine, you can take control of your well-being and live a more balanced, productive life.
supplementary_best: For chills, the best combination of herbal tinctures includes Echinacea purpurea, Astragalus membranaceus, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. Echinacea purpurea is effective in fighting off infections, as it stimulates the immune system and reduces inflammation. Astragalus membranaceus also supports immune function and helps the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to chills. Glycyrrhiza glabra adds anti-inflammatory properties and soothes the body, reducing fever and discomfort. This combination is best because it addresses multiple aspects of chills, from immune support to reducing inflammation and soothing the body.
supplementary_avoid: If you have chills, it's best to avoid certain herbal tinctures that can worsen your condition or interact with medications. For example, 'Valeriana officinalis' can cause drowsiness and slow down your body further, making your chills worse. 'Papaver somniferum' tincture should be avoided as it contains opiates, which can slow down your body's natural response to infection. 'Hydrocotyle asiatica' can also cause drowsiness and interact with other medications, such as blood thinners. 'Aconitum napellus' is highly toxic and should be avoided at all costs, as it can cause serious health problems. 'Strychnos nux-vomica' tincture is also highly toxic and should be used with caution, but it's best to avoid it if you have chills due to its toxic properties. 'Gelsemium sempervirens' can cause muscle weakness and drowsiness, which can worsen your chills. These tinctures can interact with medications or worsen your symptoms, so it's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using them.
supplementary_other_remedies: When we feel cold and shivery, our bodies are trying to fight off an infection or cold. Besides using herbal tinctures, there are other natural remedies that can help. One of these is taking a warm bath or shower. The heat from the water helps to relax our muscles and increase blood flow, which can help our body fight off the infection. This is because warm water makes our blood vessels expand, allowing more blood to flow to the affected areas and helping to bring down the fever. Another remedy is drinking warm liquids like tea or broth, which can help to soothe our throat and calm our body. The warmth also helps to loosen up mucus in our chest, making it easier to breathe. Some people also find relief by using a humidifier, which adds moisture to the air and helps to relieve congestion in our chest and sinuses. This is especially helpful when we have a cold and our nose is stuffy. Finally, getting plenty of rest and staying hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids are also important in helping our body to fight off the infection and recover from chills.
supplementary_related_ailments: When people have chills, they are often experiencing other symptoms that can be helped with medicinal herbs. One common issue is fever, which is the body's way of fighting off an infection. The herb Yarrow (Achillea millefolium) is known for its ability to lower body temperature and reduce fever.  Another problem people with chills may face is congestion, as the body's immune system works to fight off the infection. Echinacea (Echinacea spp.) is a well-known herb that can help boost the immune system and reduce congestion.  People with chills may also experience body aches and pains as their immune system fights off the infection. Willow bark (Salix spp.) contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help reduce pain and inflammation.  Additionally, some people with chills may experience digestive issues, such as diarrhea or stomach cramps, as their body's immune system works to eliminate the infection. Dandelion root (Taraxacum officinale) is a natural remedy that can help soothe digestive issues and promote overall health.  Lastly, people with chills may experience fatigue, as their body's immune system works to fight off the infection. Ginseng (Panax spp.) is an herb known for its ability to boost energy and reduce fatigue.
